name: OpenClaw AI-agent workshop should not alert as robotics in NYC
notes: This previously alerted as robotics_nyc with confidence 0.78, but the linked event is a software AI-agent workshop rather than robotics, hardware, embodied AI, or physical-world automation.

messages.text: My friend openclaw workshop is next week if you want to learn about it, I will be there if you want to meet and learn about that. Its free, just a deposit that is refunded post event to make sure there is no no show! https://example.com/openclaw-workshop

name: Generic NYC reading group should not alert as robotics
notes: A location and generic organizing language do not establish that a reading group concerns robotics.

messages.text: Could somebody take over organizing our monthly reading circle in New York?

name: Explicit NYC robotics reading group should alert
notes: The prompt should remain sensitive when the message text directly establishes both robotics and NYC.

messages.text: The NYC robotics reading group needs a new organizer for its monthly meetings.

name: Off-topic weekend chitchat should not match any topic
notes: Smoke test that the classifier does not invent matches on completely off-topic small talk in a city otherwise relevant to other topics.

messages.text: Anyone want to grab pizza in Brooklyn this Saturday? Last time was a blast.

name: Explicit fractional CTO request should match cto_coaching
notes: Direct request for fractional CTO help is a baseline match every model should catch.

messages.text: Looking for a fractional CTO to help me think through our infra rebuild over the next quarter. Anyone available or have recommendations?

name: Indirect request for engineering leadership mentorship should match cto_coaching
notes: Requires the model to read 'paid sounding board on engineering leadership' as equivalent to coaching/mentorship rather than peer chat.

messages.text: First-time CTO here. Anyone know someone who does paid sounding-board sessions on engineering leadership? Need a real sparring partner, not just peer chat.

name: Substantive leveling rubric question should match engineering_hiring
notes: Substantive practice-of-hiring discussion. Smart models should recognize a calibration/leveling rubric question as hiring-process design even without explicit hiring language.

messages.text: How are folks here drawing the line between staff and principal on the IC ladder? Our current rubric keeps producing inconsistent decisions in calibration.

name: Plain job post should not match engineering_hiring
notes: Topic description explicitly excludes job posts, recruiting announcements, referral requests, and 'we're hiring' messages. Tests that models respect the exclusion clause.

messages.text: We're hiring a Sr. Backend Engineer in NYC. $220-280k, hybrid, Go + Postgres shop. DM me if interested or know someone great.

name: Someone offering CTO coaching services should not match cto_coaching
notes: Topic is 'someone is looking for' — supply-side service offers must not invert into demand-side matches.

messages.text: I run a CTO coaching practice for Series A/B technical founders — 1:1 monthly sessions, leadership and hiring focus. Happy to share rates if useful.

name: Engineering hiring question must be isolated from off-topic batch siblings
notes: Three-message batch tests per-message isolation. Only the compensation transparency question should match; sibling messages must not pollute it or trigger spurious matches of their own.

messages.text: How do you handle compensation transparency with candidates? Do you share the band upfront or wait until offer stage?
Coffee in Williamsburg this weekend, anyone in?
Our CTO is presenting tomorrow at the TechCrunch panel, come say hi!
